Commercial Apr 8, 2026

This Airline Has The World's Best First Class In 2026

Several carriers, such as Singapore Airlines, Etihad Airways, Emirates, British Airways, and Lufthansa, continue to offer first class on key routes.

Premium cabin differentiation remains a competitive advantage for legacy carriers on long-haul routes where high-yield passengers justify dedicated first-class investment.

Aerospace Apr 8, 2026

A lunar base or a lunar economy?

We applaud the lunar base vision laid out by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman on March 24. Placing this stake in the lunar regolith is long overdue. What is missing from the mission architecture, however, is a vision for long-term economic development on the moon. It will be a profound missed...

Sustainable lunar exploration requires complementing infrastructure development with commercial economic models to attract private investment and ensure long-term viability beyond government programs.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Apr 8, 2026

LeoLabs debuts space monitoring tool for military users

LeoLabs ‘Delta’ moves beyond collision warnings to identify potential adversarial activity...

LeoLabs' shift from passive collision avoidance to active threat detection signals aerospace operators must now integrate military-grade space domain awareness into operational planning.

Safety Apr 8, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to preventing them through systematic hazard identification and continuous safety data analysis.

Commercial Apr 8, 2026

Emirates Said To Have ‘Outrageously Cheap' War Insurance Coverage

Some interesting news coming from the Financial Times as the media outlet is reporting that Emirates is paying far less for “war risk” cover than rival airlines - with information sourced from multiple insurers with direct knowledge of its policy.

Emirates' significantly lower war risk premiums suggest either superior geopolitical positioning or underpriced exposure that competitors should monitor for competitive implications.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 8, 2026

What Do Airlines Really Do With Old Planes?

The lifecycle of a commercial jet does not simply end when it is removed from a carrier's active schedule. The fate of these multi-million dollar machines is often seen as a mystery, yet understanding the retirement process is essential for grasping the true sustainability and economic scale of the...

Retired aircraft generate substantial secondary-market value through conversion, leasing, and parts harvesting, creating a complex ecosystem that extends aircraft utility beyond their original operators.

See Simple Flying →

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Industry navigates NASA’s start-and-stop approach to commercial space stations

NASA signaled last summer it planned to accelerate efforts to replace the aging International Space Station. Nearly nine months later, that push appears to have...

Inconsistent NASA funding signals for commercial space stations create planning uncertainty for private developers investing billions in competing orbital platforms.

UAVOS announces the successful implementation of an advanced manufacturing technology for its composite rotor blades:...

Core sintering technology enables stronger, lighter rotor blades with reduced manufacturing waste, directly improving UAS payload capacity and operational efficiency.

General Aviation Apr 8, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

CID's diverse traffic mix from training aircraft to air carriers enables flight schools to develop pilots in a realistic commercial environment without relocating.

Aerospace Apr 8, 2026

Artemis 2 science gets underway as Orion begins its return trip

As Artemis 2 begins its return to Earth, scientists are just starting to review the images and observations taken by the crew as they flew around the moon.

Post-mission data analysis from Artemis 2 will validate Orion's thermal protection and imaging systems critical for eventual lunar surface operations and crew safety.

The contract was awarded by the Space Development Agency for 2027 demonstrations...

Industry Apr 8, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Optimizing existing operations across airlines, airports, and airspace management can unlock significant capacity and environmental gains before introducing new aircraft technologies.

Belgium has ordered five Cessna SkyCourier turboprops for its Special Operations Forces, becoming the first military customer for the twin-engine…...

Before The Boeing 787 Vs. After: How It Changed Long-Haul Travel

The 787's composite construction and advanced systems reduced operating costs by 20% while enabling airlines to profitably operate point-to-point routes that previously required hub-and-spoke networks.

Aerospace Apr 8, 2026

Inspection cubesat demo planned as first step toward orbital defense mothership

Bulgarian satellite maker EnduroSat and British defense tech startup Shield Space aim to deploy a cubesat next year capable of maneuvering near other satellites for inspection, ahead of plans to develop a mothership that could house dozens of them for on-demand missions.

Commercial satellite operators may soon face new competition from autonomous inspection platforms that could disrupt traditional satellite servicing business models.

Teledyne FLIR boosts AI power for combat drones

Teledyne FLIR OEM is doubling down on one of the fastest-evolving areas in modern defense: smarter, more autonomous drones. The company, part of Teledyne Technologies Incorporated, has rolled out a major upgrade to its Prism SKR (“seeker”) software, transforming it from a targeting tool into a...

Enhanced AI autonomy in defense drones reduces operator workload and decision latency, fundamentally shifting targeting from human-directed to machine-assisted execution.

Astroscale passes key design milestone for UK military space-tracking cubesats

Astroscale has completed the critical design review for two cubesats slated to launch next year to help the British military monitor space weather and track objects in LEO.

Astroscale's cubesat success demonstrates how commercial space operators are becoming critical infrastructure providers for military space domain awareness capabilities.
